channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Merino the unlikely hero again - as Spain wait for Lamine Yamal show
item.description: Mikel Merino came off the bench to send Spain into the World Cup semi-finals - but Lamine Yamal is still waiting for his moment to truly shine.

item.title: Lammens' error spells end for Belgium's golden generation
item.description: Belgium's World Cup campaign ends after Senne Lammens' error against Spain - could it all be over for their golden generation too?

item.title: Is it time to give record-breaker Pickford the credit he deserves?
item.description: Is it time to finally give England keeper Jordan Pickford the credit he deserves as he enters the World Cup history books, asks chief football writer Phil McNulty.

item.title: Artificial turf & coaching revolution - how Norway shaped golden generation
item.description: Artificial turf, investment from gambling and a coaching revolution based on collaboration over egos help Norway build a golden generation of players.

item.title: England set for open-air Miami heat - but do Norway hold the edge?
item.description: England will head into unknown territory at this World Cup when they face Norway in Miami - playing in searing temperatures.

item.title: All the goals from England's 2026 World Cup campaign so far
item.description: As England gear up to face Norway at the Miami Stadium for the World Cup quarter-finals, re-live all the goals that got Thomas Tuchel's side there.

item.title: England's Guehi, Rice & James all train before Norway game
item.description: Midfielder Declan Rice and defenders Marc Guehi and Reece James all train with England on the eve of Saturday's World Cup quarter-final against Norway.

item.title: England favourites to reach semis, says Norway boss
item.description: England are favourites to reach the semi-finals of the World Cup, Norway boss Stale Solbakken has told BBC Sport.

item.title: Man Utd's Ederson move in doubt
item.description: Manchester United's attempt to complete a £35m deal for Atalanta midfielder is in doubt

item.title: Football's best bromance: Why Bellingham and Haaland break the mould
item.description: The two players have won an army of admirers from social media clips for their honesty in interviews.

item.title: Haaland has advantage over Man City team-mates
item.description: BBC Sport World Cup pundits Micah Richards, Wayne Rooney and Cesar Azpilicueta look ahead to England's match against Norway and feel that Erling Haaland will have an advantage over his Manchester City team-mates in the match.

item.title: Spain into semi-finals at expense of Belgium after Merino's late winner
item.description: Mikel Merino emerges as Spain's hero yet again as the substitute scores in the 88th minute to stun Belgium and set up a World Cup semi-final against France.

item.title: France into semi-finals after comfortable win over Morocco
item.description: Kylian Mbappe scores his eighth goal of this World Cup to make up for an earlier penalty miss with Ousmane Dembele adding a second as France prove too good for Morocco and move into the semi-finals.

item.title: 'Absolute perfection!' Mbappe fires France ahead against Morocco
item.description: All the angles of Kylian Mbappe's opener for France against Morocco in the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: When the World Cup finishes, the football continues on BBC Sport
item.description: When the World Cup ends we'll still have loads of great content every day as we build up to the new football season.

item.title: How do England stop Norway - and Haaland?
item.description: As England prepare to face Norway in a World Cup quarter-final on Saturday, how can Thomas Tuchel's side handle the main threat - star striker Erling Haaland?

item.title: Hacking worry for Argentine FA over World Cup emails
item.description: Argentina's football association signals it may have suffered a cyber attack after emails from its accounts reportedly criticised refereeing in the dramatic win against Egypt at the World Cup.

item.title: Jesus reunites with Ronaldo as new Portugal head coach
item.description: Jorge Jesus is named Portugal's new head coach on a four-year contract to run through to the 2030 World Cup.

item.title: Salah demands put Saudi switch in doubt - Saturday's gossip
item.description: Mohamed Salah's financial demands putting off Saudi clubs, Bayern Munich rebuff Real Madrid move for Michael Olise, Barcelona agree to sign Karim Adeyemi, plus more.

item.title: Edwards leaves football chief role with Liverpool owners FSG
item.description: Liverpool owners Fenway Sports Group announce the departure of Michael Edwards, the company's chief executive of football.

item.title: Mexico boss' explicit heckle a bit of fun - Gordon
item.description: England winger Anthony Gordon says being sworn at by Mexico boss Javier Aguirre in a viral moment of their World Cup match was "just a bit of fun".

item.title: Burnley appoint Hayen as new head coach
item.description: Burnley appoint Genk boss Nicky Hayen as their new head coach on a three-year deal.

item.title: New species named after Cape Verde keeper Vozinha
item.description: A new species of sea slug has been named after Cape Verde goalkeeper Vozinha to mark his performance against Spain at the World Cup.

item.title: Coroner rules inquest needed into Nobby Stiles' death
item.description: An inquest into his death will be held after a corner finds injury consistent with repeated head traumas.

item.title: England defender Charles joins Man City from Chelsea
item.description: Manchester City have signed England international Niamh Charles for £500,000 from Women's Super League rivals Chelsea.

item.title: A-Z World Cup quiz: Name the best performing team for every letter
item.description: When it comes to the World Cup it's not all about the numbers - the letters are important too!

item.title: Loyal and royal: How the future King became a football superfan
item.description: A love of football connects William to the country he will one day reign over in a way polo would not.

item.title: 'Influential' Lamine Yamal has 'more to give' to Spain
item.description: BBC Sport World Cup pundits Cesar Azpilicueta and Wayne Rooney look at how Lamine Yamal performed against Belgium and feel there is "more to come" from the Spain forward.

item.title: 'World Cup quarter-final is Norway's biggest event ever by far'
item.description: Norwegian pundits look ahead to their World Cup quarter-final against England, which has been described as the "biggest event ever in Norway".

item.title: Watch every penalty from Switzerland v Colombia shootout
item.description: Watch the full penalties as Switzerland beat Colombia 4-3 in the shootout to advance to the quarter-finals.

item.title: Argentina make incredible late comeback to beat Egypt
item.description: Argentina survive an almighty scare before delivering a scarcely believable second-half comeback to break Egypt hearts and reach the quarter-finals of the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Switzerland beat Colombia on penalties to reach quarter-finals
item.description: Gregor Kobel is Switzerland's penalty hero, after pulling off a stunning shootout save from Colombia's Cucho Hernandez to help set up a quarter-final clash with Argentina.

item.title: VAR controversially rules out Egypt goal
item.description: Mostafa Zico has his goal for Egypt ruled out following a VAR review, for a foul on Lisandro Martinez in the build up, during their World Cup last 16 match in Atlanta.

item.title: Fernandez header seals dramatic Argentina comeback
item.description: Enzo Fernandez scores late to give Argentina the lead against Egypt in their last 16 match at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Messi rescues Argentina with equaliser against Egypt
item.description: Lionel Messi scores a late equaliser for Argentina against Egypt to level the game at 2-2 during their match in Atlanta.

item.title: Are stuttering penalties clever or a fail? Stats suggest they are a no-no
item.description: Kylian Mbappe's penalty miss in France's win against Morocco is the latest example of a player missing a penalty after stuttering in the run-up.

item.title: How good is Monga and what role could he play at Man City?
item.description: Manchester City appear to have struck an early blow on Arsenal by agreeing a deal to sign teenage starlet Jeremy Monga from Leicester City.

item.title: Arsenal sign Spain defender Batlle on free transfer
item.description: Arsenal sign Spain international Ona Batlle on a free transfer following her exit from Barcelona.

item.title: Haaland & Heggem inspiring Baggies' striker Heggebo
item.description: West Bromwich Albion striker Aune Heggebo says watching Erling Haaland and his old Baggies team-mate Torbjorn Heggem exploits for Norway at the World Cup has been amazing.

item.title: France cruise into semi-finals - are they best-ever version of Les Bleus?
item.description: World Cup winners in 1998 and 2018, France have created another special team and in beating Morocco showed why they are favourites to win the 2026 competition.

item.title: Man City's Lewis on faith, football and helping the homeless
item.description: Manchester City midfielder Rico Lewis talks about finding God and his charity work involving football that is aiming to tackle homelessness.

item.title: The wild hip-hop parties that started Tuchel's journey to England boss
item.description: Thomas Tuchel is bidding to become the first foreign manager to win the World Cup. BBC Sport charts the start of his journey to becoming England boss.

item.title: Born in Leeds, crafted in Norway - Haaland's worlds about to collide
item.description: Erling Haaland has guided Norway to their first World Cup since 1998. So what is it like being a global name from a nation with a population of just 5.5 million?

item.title: Duran completes switch to Celtic from Qarabag
item.description: Celtic confirm the signing of Colombian forward Camilo Duran from Qarabag on a five-year deal.

item.title: Klopp reveals how Liverpool tried to sign Mbappe
item.description: Jurgen Klopp says he "flew around in a circle" in a private plane with Kylian Mbappe and his family when he tried to sign him for Liverpool a decade ago.

item.title: What's going on at Newcastle with Guimaraes' future in doubt?
item.description: Bruno Guimaraes joined Newcastle in January 2022 and hoped to fulfil all his ambitions at the club. If he leaves to join Arsenal, what does it mean for the Magpies' direction?

item.title: How football has helped immigrants make a home in the US
item.description: Football is the common bond for the immigrants and refugees who play matches in a Maine park, and has helped them develop a sense of community in a foreign land.

item.title: I've changed my mind, England could actually win this thing - Shearer
item.description: Alan Shearer explains why he now has belief and confidence that England can go further in this World Cup than he thought.

item.title: BBC expert Sutton's World Cup quarter-final predictions
item.description: BBC Sport's football expert Chris Sutton gives his predictions for the quarter-final ties at the 2026 World Cup, including England against Norway.

item.title: Premier League pre-season - who are your club playing?
item.description: The World Cup is still going on but Premier League clubs will soon begin their pre-season friendlies - who is your club taking on and when?

item.title: Late goals, comebacks and upsets - is record-breaking World Cup best ever?
item.description: What a World Cup it has been with great goals, thrilling comebacks, late drama, shock results. But how will this tournament be remembered?

item.title: A striker duel for the ages & Qatar revenge - what to expect in quarter-finals
item.description: Setting the scene for each of the four intriguing World Cup quarter-finals that begin on Thursday.

item.title: From Barcelona to Bromley: Inside Putellas' move to London City
item.description: London City Lionesses have pulled off one of the biggest signings in WSL history - but how did they persuade Alexia Putellas to join?

item.title: A shambolic end for the American dream - did Balogun saga play part?
item.description: Mauricio Pochettino says he feels "disappointed with too many people" who brought "politics and manipulation" into the game as the US exit World Cup.

item.title: All done deals in June 2026
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: What are the key dates for the 2026-27 football season?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything explains the full list of key dates for the 2026-27 men's football season

item.title: Should teams bring on players just for penalty shootouts?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at the numbers behind late substitutes taking penalties in shootouts.

item.title: Who has been eliminated from the World Cup?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team lists the full set of nations who have been eliminated from the 2026 World Cup so far

item.title: How are the TV picks decided for the World Cup's round of 32?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains the complexities behind television selections for the 2026 World Cup's round of 32 fixtures

item.title: Is five-match ban for Qatar's Madibo excessive?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team look at whether Madibo's punishment for his challenge on Kone was proportionate.

item.title: What is the World Cup prize money?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team break down the prize money on offer at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Who is the African World Cup goalscorer older than Ronaldo and Messi?
item.description: While Cristiano Ronaldo and Lionel Messi appear to be in a personal battle to rewrite the World Cup record books, there is one landmark that appears beyond them.

item.title: Devlin attracts interest from Rangers - gossip
item.description: Rangers linked with midfielders as English clubs express interest in Scottish left-back.

item.title: Aberdeen sell Keskinen to Odense after 'very good offer'
item.description: Aberdeen sell Topi Keskinen to Odense for an undisclosed fee, which Pittodrie manager Stephen Robinson describes as "a very good offer".

item.title: 'A bit of a rebel' - what can Celtic expect from forward Duran?
item.description: Celtic are set to sign their first player in the transfer window - 24-year-old Colombian forward Camilo Duran from Qarabag - for a reported £5.5m. But who is he?

item.title: What are the games to look out for in the Scottish League Cup?
item.description: Four top-flight clubs open their competitive season in the group stages of the Scottish League Cup on Saturday.

item.title: West Ham sign forward Salmon on two-year deal
item.description: West Ham have signed England forward Ebony Salmon on a two-year deal following her exit from Aston Villa.

item.title: Sunderland women appoint FA's Gray as CEO
item.description: Sunderland Women appoint James Gray as their new chief executive officer following the recent takeover by Bay Collective.

item.title: Maddy Cusack inquest adjourned over evidence request
item.description: Maddy Cusack died after being found unconscious at her family home in September 2023.

item.title: Arsenal sign Switzerland midfielder Reuteler
item.description: Arsenal sign Switzerland midfielder Geraldine Reuteler following her exit from Eintracht Frankfurt.

item.title: In spotlight but shunning limelight - Olise's unique rise to top
item.description: A footballer who dislikes too much media attention, but Michael Olise might not have much choice if he carries on his World Cup heroics.

item.title: How Cornish miners brought football to Mexico
item.description: As Mexico prepares to become the first country to host or co-host the World Cup three times, BBC Sport looks at its Cornish footballing roots.

item.title: The summer a golden generation lost its shine
item.description: The summer of 2006 was meant to be England's moment. A world-class squad, celebrity partners and confident fans - the hype was huge and the fall was hard.

item.title: How Toone is navigating grief through football
item.description: England and Manchester United midfielder Ella Toone opens up about grief, football and preparing for her wedding without her dad in a new BBC documentary.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.
